title: Elastic (ESTC) Q3 2026 Earnings Call Summary
text: Elastic (ESTC) Q3 2026 Earnings Call Summary. Key Financial Metrics: Total revenue $450 million, up 18% YoY (16% constant currency). Sales-led subscription revenue $376 million, up 21% YoY (19% constant currency). Subscription gross margin 82%, total gross margin 78%. Non-GAAP operating margin 18.6%. Adjusted free cash flow ~$54 million (12% margin). Current remaining performance obligations (CRPO) ~$1.06 billion, up 19% (15% constant currency) — first time exceeding $1 billion. Remaining performance obligations (RPO) up 22% YoY (18% constant currency). $100,000+ ACV customers 1,660+, up 14% with ~60 sequential net additions. Large deal commitments ($1M+ annual value) grew over 30%. AI Adoption Highlights: Over 3,000 customers using Elastic for AI use cases. 2,700+ customers on Elastic Cloud for vector database workloads. 470+ high-value customers ($100,000+ ACV) using AI solutions. 410+ using specifically for vector database functionality. 28% AI penetration within $100,000+ ACV customer cohort. CEO emphasized ability to bridge enterprise data to the LLM with platform directly translating into expanded AI adoption. Product Innovation: Agent Builder (general availability) for AI agent development. Elastic Workflows (technical preview) for automation capability. Elasticsearch GPU plugin (technical preview) for 12x faster indexing acceleration. GINA AI integration with multilingual re-ranking models. Cloud Connect for on-premises customers to leverage Elastic Cloud GPUs. Vector search RAM requirements reduced by two orders of magnitude through innovations like better binary quantization (BBQ), making Elasticsearch vector search up to 8x faster than OpenSearch. Management Guidance: Q4 FY2026 guidance: Total revenue $445–$447 million (15% growth at midpoint). Sales-led subscription revenue $371–$373 million (18% growth midpoint). Non-GAAP operating margin ~14.5%. Full-Year FY2026 Guidance (Raised): Total revenue $1.734–$1.736 billion (17% growth midpoint). Sales-led subscription revenue $1.434–$1.436 billion (20% growth midpoint). Non-GAAP operating margin 16.3%. $186 million returned to shareholders in Q3; 60% of $500M repurchase program completed. Q4 headwind of $14–$15 million due to three fewer calendar days. Management characterized frontier LLMs as operating systems of tomorrow requiring data platforms to deliver context.
